core_reading_principles: observe the surface before interpreting it
prefer system description over brand essence claims
prefer repeated signals over isolated impressions
treat absence as a signal when it is meaningful

perceptual_vocabulary.meaning: how much the brand suppresses noise, decoration, or overstatement
how quickly the surface becomes identifiable from recurring cues
how forgiving, calm, or tactically gentle the experience feels
how much uncontrolled variation or disorder appears across the surface
how much repeated structure creates a learnable habit
how much meaning is packed into a small number of cues
how quickly the surface feels overused, exhausted, or visually stale
the visible cues that try to produce a premium reading
how well the parts behave like one designed system
the use of spectacle, announcement energy, or exaggerated reveal patterns
the amount of effort required to move through the surface
how fixed, narrow, or over-controlled the identity system feels
how much the surface leaves open space, restraint, or unclaimed territory
how stable the pacing, rhythm, and release pattern feels over time

hierarchy: what is pushed into the background
whether hierarchy guides attention or scatters it
spacing: whether spacing is generous, compressed, padded, or unstable
whether spacing creates calm or friction
whether the spacing system repeats consistently
typography: whether the typographic voice is restrained or performative
whether type behaves like a system or like decoration
whether type supports legibility, rhythm, and hierarchy
motion: whether motion reinforces the system or distracts from it
whether motion is purposeful, theatrical, delayed, abrupt, or absent
whether motion creates continuity or interruption
cadence: whether the brand reveals itself in a stable rhythm or in bursts
whether cadence is consistent across channels and time
repetition: whether repetition builds recognition or produces monotony
whether repetition is intentional or residual
navigation: whether the path through the surface is clear, compressed, or fragmented
whether navigation reinforces identity or hides it
whether the system teaches the user how it wants to be read
launch_patterns: whether launches are theatrical or quiet
whether launches feel systemized or episodic
whether launches reinforce a recurring identity pattern or reset it
visual_rhythm: whether the surface has rhythm and what kind
whether the rhythm is dense, sparse, fast, slow, jagged, or smooth

narrative_interpretation_rules: name the pattern in concrete terms
infer only what the evidence reasonably supports
avoid turning a surface reading into a business-school conclusion
forbidden_narrative_behaviors: filler transitions that do not add evidence
